title: quassia crustacea
intro: Quassia crustacea is a tropical evergreen tree native to the Caribbean region, also known as the "yellow dogwood". Its bark and roots contain a powerful compound called quassin, which has been used for centuries in traditional medicine to treat a variety of ailments. The medicinal properties of Quassia crustacea include its ability to act as an antibacterial, antifungal, and antiparasitic agent, making it effective in treating digestive issues, fever, and skin conditions. Quassia crustacea can be prepared as a tincture, infusion, or powder, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote digestion, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system.
